summary: Execute a structured literature review with systematic search, screening, data extraction, and narrative synthesis following PRISMA-aligned methodology.

inputs.name: review_question
inputs.description: The research question or topic to review.

inputs.name: search_strategy
inputs.description: Databases, search terms, and Boolean operators to use.

inputs.name: inclusion_criteria
inputs.description: Criteria for including and excluding studies.

outputs.name: prisma_flow
outputs.description: PRISMA flow diagram showing record counts at each stage.

outputs.name: evidence_table
outputs.description: Structured table of included studies with key characteristics.

outputs.name: narrative_synthesis
outputs.description: Narrative summary of findings organized by theme.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is a literature review, not a code review or financial analysis.
Require a documented search strategy before executing searches.
Verify inclusion criteria were applied consistently across all records.
Synthesis must distinguish well-supported from weakly-supported findings.

steps.action: Define review protocol with objectives, search strategy, and eligibility criteria.
Execute systematic search across specified databases.
Screen titles, abstracts, and full texts against inclusion criteria.
Extract key data from included studies into a structured table.
Produce a narrative synthesis organized by theme with evidence quality grading.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read local review protocols and prior review data.

tools.name: web.search
tools.purpose: Search academic databases for relevant literature.

tools.name: web.fetch
tools.purpose: Fetch abstracts and full texts from sources.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Save review outputs including evidence tables and synthesis.

verification.method: Verify search strategy is fully documented with terms and databases.
Verify inclusion criteria applied consistently across all records.
Verify synthesis claims are supported by extracted data.

success_criteria: The review follows a documented and reproducible search strategy.
All included studies are described in a structured evidence table.
The synthesis distinguishes strong from weak evidence.

rollback.when: If inclusion criteria were applied inconsistently, restart screening from the protocol.
